# UC-22.11.68 · Log Retention Index Frozen Bucket Age Compliance

## Description

Reports oldest searchable bucket age for indexes storing PCI audit evidence so at least twelve months of immediately available history exists before cold or frozen archives.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Adjust `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` to ≥395 days per policy (allow leap buffer). (2) Document frozen/archive retrieval for older periods. (3) Alert if `disabled=1` on PCI indexes. (4) Quarterly compliance sign-off screenshot.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `rest` search against Splunk cluster manager or monitoring console.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `| rest /services/data/indexes` — `title`, `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`, `totalEventCount`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Adjust `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` to ≥395 days per policy (allow leap buffer). (2) Document frozen/archive retrieval for older periods. (3) Alert if `disabled=1` on PCI indexes. (4) Quarterly compliance sign-off screenshot.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| where match(title,"^(pci|cde|payment)_")
| eval retain_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,2)
| where retain_days<395
| table title, retain_days, totalEventCount, disabled
| sort retain_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**Log Retention Index Frozen Bucket Age Compliance (PCI DSS Req 10.5.1, 10.5.1.2)** — Reports oldest searchable bucket age for indexes storing PCI audit evidence so at least twelve months of immediately available history exists before cold or frozen archives.

Documented **Data sources**: `| rest /services/data/indexes` — `title`, `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`, `totalEventCount`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `rest` search against Splunk cluster manager or monitoring console. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(title,"^(pci|cde|payment)_")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **retain_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where retain_days<395` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: disabled, retain_days, title, totalEventCount.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (index, retain_days), gauge (min retain_days), single value (indexes under policy).

## SPL

```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| where match(title,"^(pci|cde|payment)_")
| eval retain_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,2)
| where retain_days<395
| table title, retain_days, totalEventCount, disabled
| sort retain_days
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (index, retain_days), gauge (min retain_days), single value (indexes under policy).
